title: Return of the Jedi 4
content:

'''''Return of the Jedi'' 4''' is the fourth issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983)|Star Wars: Return of the Jedi]]'' mini-series, [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']]' four issue comic adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']].

There are five pin-up pages in this issue, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] & [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] by [[Rick J. Bryant]] & [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] & [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] by [[Bill Sienkiewicz]].



==Publisher's summary==
''If you're one of the two or three people out there who doesn't know the ending to this latest, greatest STAR WARS epic — or even if you do — you'll still enjoy the conclusion to the most sensational space saga of all!''<ref name="MA7" />

==Plot summary==
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] attempt to destroy the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] while [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] duels with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

==Continuity==
Unlike the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel comic]] adaptations of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] & [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], this was not part of the regular series.

The [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] who steals the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] is incorrectly identified as [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] in the story but it is actually [[Paploo/Legends|Paploo]].
